session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9186 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified PR argument.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [195 lines omitted] ...
131→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 133→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 171→ "error": null 185→## Error Case 199→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 217→ "error": null

## ✅ Test Coverage Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-10 11:42:13 PT

No source additions or significant modifications detected. This PR is a pure refactoring that renames variables from `plan_number`/`plan_id` to `pr_number`/`pr_id` across 47 source files and 7 test files. All changes are variable/parameter renames with no logic changes or new functionality.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Refactoring Summary
- **47 source files modified**: Variable and parameter renaming only
- **7 test files modified**: Corresponding test updates to match renamed variables
- **No new source files added**
- **No deleted source files**
- **All tests pass**: Existing test coverage maintained

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-10 [Current]**: Pure refactoring PR - renamed ~400 variable occurrences from plan_* to pr_*, all tests passing and updated accordingly

</details>", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9186#issuecomment-4033638144" }, { "id": 4033639061, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": "<!-- tripwires-review -->

## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-10 11:42:28 PT

No tripwire violations detected. Clean terminology standardization refactor (plan_number→pr_number, plan_id→pr_id). All 57 files updated consistently with no behavioral changes.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered

None - This is a pure variable renaming refactor.

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches (Mechanical)

✅ `subprocess\.run\(` - None found
✅ `run_subprocess_with_context\(` - None found
✅ `Path\.home\(\)` - None found
✅ `import time` - None found
✅ `time\.sleep\(` - None found
✅ `datetime\.now\(` - None found
✅ `os\.chdir\(` - None found
✅ `gt\s+(sync|submit|restack|create|modify)` - None found
✅ `try:|\bexcept\s` in fake.py/dry_run.py - None found

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches

✅ Gateway signature changes - None
✅ New gateway methods - None
✅ Error handling pattern changes - None
✅ Test file modifications - None (no test files in diff)

### Violations Summary

No violations found.

### Files Reviewed

**Excluded from review:**
- `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` (skill documentation - per exclusion rules)

**Reviewed (55 files):**
- All files contain only variable/parameter renaming (plan_number→pr_number, plan_id→pr_id)
- No behavioral changes, no new code patterns, no tripwire triggers
- Consistent application of terminology across all modified files

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-10 11:42:28 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Clean terminology standardization refactor (plan_number→pr_number, plan_id→pr_id). All 57 files updated consistently with no behavioral changes.

... [15 lines omitted] ...
"body": "<!-- dignified-python-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-10 11:45:02 PT

Found 0 violations. All changes comply with dignified Python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL over EAFP - No try/except control flow violations
✅ Exception handling - Compliant patterns
✅ Path operations - Correct exists() checks
✅ Import organization - No inline imports or re-exports
✅ Default parameters - None introduced in main code
✅ Function signatures - All 5+ parameter functions properly structured
✅ Subprocess usage - All use check=True

### Scope
This is a mechanical refactoring PR that renames variables for clarity:
- `plan_number` → `pr_number`
- `plan_id` → `pr_id`
- `plan_title` → `pr_title`
- `extract_plan_title()` → `extract_pr_title()`

Additionally, one minor improvement clarifies duplicate PR number usage in `admin.py::test_plan_implement_gh_workflow()` by renaming the draft PR variable from `pr_number` to `draft_pr_number`.

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/admin.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/consolidate_learn_plans_dispatch.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/add_plan_label.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/add_plan_labels.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/close_pr.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/close_prs.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/create_impl_context_from_plan.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/detect_plan_from_branch.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/exit_plan_mode_hook.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/learn/learn_cmd.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/objective_helpers.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot/cli.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/check_cmd.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/checkout_cmd.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/close_cmd.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/duplicate_check_cmd.py`: Compliant

- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view/cli.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view/operation.py`: Compliant

- `src/erk/cli/commands/wt/delete_cmd.py`: Compliant
- `src/erk/core/file_utils.py`: Compliant (pre-existing default parameters not modified)
- `tests/commands/learn/test_display.py`: Compliant
- `tests/commands/pr/test_check_plan.py`: Compliant
- `tests/core/utils/test_file_utils.py`: Compliant

session.assistant: Now I'll get the git diff context to check for file restructuring:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Get diff stats for file restructuring analysis

session.assistant: Now I'll analyze the PR feedback data and classify it according to the skill's guidelines:
session.tool_result:
55→## Comment Classification Model 56→ 57→For each comment, determine: 58→ 59→### Classification 60→ 61→Classification determines how the thread is presented to the user, not whether it appears. 62→ 63→- **Actionable**: Code changes requested, violations to fix, missing tests, documentation updates requested, bot suggestions to add tests, bot style/refactoring suggestions (optional/could) 64→- **Informational**: CI-generated style suggestions, acknowledgments on review threads 65→ 66→**Important:** Every unresolved review thread goes into `actionable_threads`, regardless of whether it's from a bot or human. The `classification` field distinguishes how the user should handle it. 67→ 68→Discussion comments that are purely informational (CI status updates, Graphite stack comments, PR description summaries) are still counted in `informational_count` and do NOT appear in `actionable_threads`. 69→ 70→### Pre-Existing Detection 71→ 72→For each thread in `actionable_threads`, determine the `pre_existing` field: 73→ 74→- **`pre_existing: true`** when ALL of: 75→ 1. Author is a bot (`[bot]` suffix) 76→ 2. PR involves file restructuring (renames, splits, moves visible in `git diff --stat -M -C`) 77→ 3. The flagged pattern would have been equally flaggable in the original file location (generic code quality issue, not specific to the restructuring) 78→ 79→- **`pre_existing: false`** when ANY of: 80→ - Author is human 81→ - The issue is specifically caused by the restructuring (e.g., `__all__` in a new `__init__.py`, new import paths) 82→ - No restructuring detected in the PR 83→ 84→### Complexity
... [45 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Based on my analysis of PR 9186, I'll now generate the complete classified feedback JSON:
## Analysis Summary
The PR has: - **No unresolved review threads** (review_threads is empty) - **6 discussion comments** from bots and humans regarding: 1. Plan queued for implementation (human - informational) 2. GitHub Action workflow started (bot - informational) 3. Test coverage review (bot - informational, no new code) 4. Tripwires review (bot - informational, no violations) 5. Dignified code simplifier review (bot - informational, 2 violations noted inline) 6. Dignified Python review (bot - informational, 0 violations)
The PR is a pure variable renaming refactoring with no file restructuring (diff shows only line count changes, no M, C, or D flags for moves/copies/deletes). All discussion comments are CI-generated status updates or informational messages that do NOT appear in `actionable_threads`.
The dignified code simplifier review mentions 2 violations but these are posted as inline comments (not separate review threads), so they would be handled as part of the implementation workflow.
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 9186, "pr_title": "Sweep plan_number/plan_id refs to pr_number/pr_id in exec scripts and remaining leaf commands", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9186", "actionable_threads": [], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 6, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
